title: Cryptgeon
description: Secure, open-source service for sharing self-destructing, end-to-end-encrypted notes and files, inspired by PrivNote.
installation: Deploy with the bundled Valkey (Redis-compatible) add-on, which is where all encrypted notes/files are stored temporarily until they expire or are viewed; nothing is persisted on disk, so no volume is required. The app connects to Valkey via the REDIS env var (passwordless in-cluster). Encryption happens client-side in the browser using AES-GCM, so the server never sees plaintext or keys and there is no admin login or user database to seed. Tune SIZE_LIMIT (max payload, e.g. 4 MiB), MAX_VIEWS and MAX_EXPIRATION (minutes) to taste. Because data lives only in Valkey memory, restarting/redeploying Valkey clears all outstanding notes. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is needed.
